source: Indiana Department of Insurance · 2022 Homeowner Complaint Index
methodology: Complaint Index = (company's share of complaints) / (company's share of premiums). 1.0 = average. <1 better than average, >1 worse. For carrier groups with multiple subsidiaries we report the volume-weighted blend of the homeowners-line subsidiaries.

interpretation.≤0.50: excellent — half the complaints expected for size

State Farm.note: State Farm's huge book makes 1.08 (essentially average) the structural ceiling. 39 complaints absolute.

USAA.note: USAA Cas Ins Co subsidiary 1.42 — main parent USAA shows 0.50. Bundled member-only carrier.

Travelers.note: Travelers Personal 0.27 + Travco 0.62 — both well below average. Confirmed by NAIC's national 'Travelers ~half the average' callout.

Allstate.note: Allstate Ind Co subsidiary 6.93 (small premium pool but a worrying outlier). Main subsidiary 1.26. Consistent with the Weiss 50.9% close-without-payment finding cited on predictor.html.

Erie.note: Erie Ins Co 0.26 + Erie Ins Exch 1.90. Volume-weighted blend favors the bigger Erie Ins Co subsidiary.

Liberty Mutual.note: Below Indiana's $1M premium reporting threshold for homeowners line — small footprint.

Progressive.note: Progressive doesn't underwrite homeowners directly — partners with American Strategic / ASI (2.50 index). Mismatch between auto brand and homeowners service is well-documented.

Farmers.note: Farmers Ins Exch 0.59 + Grp 3.09 + Prop 2.39 — volume-weighted blend.

The Hartford.note: Premiums under Indiana's $1M reporting threshold for HO line.

Encompass.note: Allstate subsidiary — even higher complaint index than parent.

Lemonade.note: Newer / small homeowners footprint — not in Indiana's 2022 report. Anecdotally low complaints per NAIC national data.

Geico.note: Geico doesn't directly underwrite homeowners — partners with ASI/Liberty Mutual.

AMIG (Cincinnati Financial).note: Cincinnati Insurance broadly: ultra-low complaint rate per NAIC national. Specific 2022 IN data unavailable in this report.

Chubb.note: Luxury/HNW segment; small homeowner footprint in mass-market IN data.

Assurant.note: InsuranceNewsNet 2024: 'American Banker, an affiliate of Assurant, had the highest complaint score among homeowners'. No specific Indiana 2022 entry.
